matrix.description: Compare stock and tuned descriptions for the Zymtrace MCP profiling-analysis tools.

instruction_variants.instructions: Choose the single Zymtrace MCP tool that should be called next. The live server also exposes MCP resources named topfunctions, topentities, and flamegraph; in a resource-capable host, read those resources before calling the same-named tools, but in this tool-only matrix choose the fallback tool. Use get_date_time when the user gives relative time or omits a range, because omitted ranges default to the last 1 hour and generated REST-style project APIs require Unix milliseconds. Unless the user explicitly asks for multiple projects or names a different project, project-scoped tools should use the default project_id 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000. Use projects_search only when the user asks to list/find/search/switch projects or names a non-default project without its UUID. Prefer high-level tools for common profiling questions: topfunctions for hot functions, topentities for hot executables/scripts/hosts/threads/containers/pods/apps, hot_traces for stack-trace/call-tree discovery, flamegraph for a rendered flamegraph fallback, and recommendations for already-generated optimization advice. Use project_metrics_activity_aggr before project_metrics_query, especially for GPU/inference metrics discovery. Use raw project event/stat endpoints only when the user has a project scope and asks for low-level aggregate/raw event payloads.
instruction_variants.name: zymtrace_host_rules

instruction_variants.instructions: Apply the Zymtrace MCP server rules plus the Zymtrace optimization skills. CPU-only or entity-only investigations default to CPU. Rank-first CPU requests such as what is eating CPU, which app/container/pod/process is hottest, or biggest ROI should start with topentities or topfunctions, then drill into the selected user-owned entry with hot_traces. GPU, CUDA, inference, vLLM, SGLang, Triton, training, or accelerator requests use the GPU workflow: discover matching GPU/framework/CPU metrics first with project_metrics_activity_aggr, then query metric values when names are known, then pull the GPU hot_traces view and the matching CPU hot_traces view with the same filters. For call-tree or flamegraph analysis on CPU, GPU, or ALLOC, prefer hot_traces when available; use flamegraph only as a fallback or when the user explicitly wants rendered flamegraph data. First hot_traces calls must use meta_only=true and limit<=5; full trace fetches require a selected prefix_hash and should use limit=1. For allocation or memory-profile requests use profile_type ALLOC. Exclude zymtrace-profiler from ranking targets.
instruction_variants.name: zymtrace_host_and_skill_rules

matrix.name: zymtrace mcp tool-selection matrix

tools.name: flamegraph
tools.purpose: Return profiling data as flamegraph.

tools.name: get_date_time
tools.purpose: Get the current local date/time and machine-usable Unix milliseconds.

tools.name: hot_traces
tools.purpose: Find hot CPU/GPU profiling stack traces.

tools.name: project_events_raw
tools.purpose: Fetch raw events.

tools.name: project_flamegraph_json
tools.purpose: Generate a JSON-format flamegraph.

tools.name: project_metrics_activity_aggr
tools.purpose: Retrieve metrics activity aggregations for a project.

tools.name: project_metrics_query
tools.purpose: Query metrics for a project with custom filters.

tools.name: projects_events_aggregate
tools.purpose: Aggregate project events.

tools.name: projects_events_aggregate_host_metadata
tools.purpose: Aggregate host metadata.

tools.name: projects_events_aggregate_top_field_values
tools.purpose: Return the most common values for common filter fields.

tools.name: projects_search
tools.purpose: Search projects.

tools.name: projects_stats_aggregate_top_architectures
tools.purpose: Return top architectures.

tools.name: projects_stats_aggregate_top_clusters
tools.purpose: Return top clusters.

tools.name: projects_stats_aggregate_top_containers
tools.purpose: Return top containers.

tools.name: projects_stats_aggregate_top_deployments
tools.purpose: Return top deployments.

tools.name: projects_stats_aggregate_top_hosts
tools.purpose: Return top hosts.

tools.name: projects_stats_aggregate_top_main_exes
tools.purpose: Return top main executables.

tools.name: projects_stats_aggregate_top_namespaces
tools.purpose: Return top namespaces.

tools.name: projects_stats_aggregate_top_pods
tools.purpose: Return top pods.

tools.name: projects_stats_aggregate_top_script_names
tools.purpose: Return top script names.

tools.name: projects_stats_aggregate_top_thread_names
tools.purpose: Return top thread names.

tools.name: projects_stats_aggregate_top_user_tags
tools.purpose: Return top user tags.

tools.name: recommendations
tools.purpose: Return recommendations already generated for a project's hot traces.

properties.entity: executable, script, host, thread, deployment, container, namespace, or pod

tools.name: topentities
tools.purpose: Return list of GPU, CPU or allocation top entities.

tools.name: topfunctions
tools.purpose: Return list of GPU, CPU or allocation top functions.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid when the user asks for ranked functions, entities, generated recommendations, raw event aggregates, or a call-tree drilldown that should start with hot_traces.
tools.name: flamegraph
tools.purpose: Return a high-level rendered flamegraph fallback for a time window and optional runtime filters.
quality_checks: Use the MCP flamegraph resource first when the host supports resources.
Use CPU unless the user asks for GPU or allocation data.
Include a bounded time window.
Apply named entity filters instead of fetching unfiltered data.
tools.use_when: Use when the user explicitly asks for rendered flamegraph data and hot_traces is not the better call-tree first step.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid when the user already supplied absolute RFC3339 timestamps or Unix millisecond timestamps.
tools.name: get_date_time
tools.purpose: Get current local time and Unix milliseconds for relative or omitted Zymtrace time-window requests.
quality_checks: Use before REST-style project tools that require start_ts/end_ts in Unix milliseconds.
Use when the range is omitted so the default last-1-hour window can be calculated.
Do not hand-convert relative dates when this tool is available.
tools.use_when: Use when the user says now, last hour, today, yesterday, recent, or gives no time range.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid as a first full-stack fetch. Use meta_only=true and a small limit before requesting a selected prefix_hash.

properties.meta_only: Use true for discovery; false only after selecting a prefix_hash or when full stacks are explicit

tools.name: hot_traces
tools.purpose: Discover hot stack traces/call trees and drill into one selected trace.
quality_checks: First call uses meta_only=true unless the user explicitly asks for full stacks.
Default limit is 5 or lower.
Use filter_text or structured filters when the user names an entity.
For full drilldown, set prefix_hash, meta_only=false, and limit=1.
tools.use_when: Use when the user asks what trace, stack, call tree, call path, or GPU/CPU flamegraph evidence is hot, or asks to drill into a specific hot trace.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid for ordinary top functions, top entities, metrics, recommendations, or summaries. Prefer higher-level tools first.

properties.request_body: Raw event filter with start_ts/end_ts and approx_limit

tools.name: project_events_raw
tools.purpose: Fetch raw profiling events for one project scope.
quality_checks: Use project_id 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000 when the user did not name a different project.
Keep approx_limit small.
Use get_date_time first for relative time ranges.
tools.use_when: Use when the user explicitly asks for raw events or event samples.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid when the user only wants a high-level rendered flamegraph; use flamegraph resource/tool or hot_traces for call-tree analysis.
tools.name: project_flamegraph_json
tools.purpose: Generate project-specific JSON flamegraph data for downstream processing.
quality_checks: Use project_id 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000 when the user did not name a different project.
Use when JSON is needed, not just a rendered flamegraph.
tools.use_when: Use when the user asks for JSON flamegraph data from a project scope.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid when the metric name is already known and the user asks for values; use project_metrics_query then.
tools.name: project_metrics_activity_aggr
tools.purpose: Discover active metric names and useful attributes for a project, including GPU, CPU, model, service, token, latency, and framework metrics.
quality_checks: Use project_id 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000 when the user did not name a different project.
Use before metric value queries.
Return all relevant metric names and dimensions instead of choosing one representative metric.
Start GPU and inference investigations here before pulling flamegraphs or hot traces.
tools.use_when: Use when the user asks what metrics exist, which model/service/token/GPU/framework metrics are active, or when a GPU/inference workflow needs metrics-first context.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid before discovering metric names when the user is unsure what metrics exist.
tools.name: project_metrics_query
tools.purpose: Query metric time-series values for known metric names and dimensions.
quality_checks: Use project_id 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000 when the user did not name a different project.
Use project_metrics_activity_aggr first when metric names are unknown.
Include every relevant discovered metric instead of picking one representative metric.
tools.use_when: Use when the user asks for metric values, latency series, token metrics, gauges, counters, histograms, GPU utilization, memory, SM efficiency, or inference framework metrics for a known metric name.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid for top dimensions that have a dedicated stats endpoint or for raw event samples.
tools.name: projects_events_aggregate
tools.purpose: Aggregate profiling events for one project scope.
quality_checks: Use project_id 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000 when the user did not name a different project.
Use time filters and event kind filters.
tools.use_when: Use when the user asks for event aggregate counts or grouped event summaries.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid for generic top hosts; use projects_stats_aggregate_top_hosts for host ranking.
tools.name: projects_events_aggregate_host_metadata
tools.purpose: Aggregate host metadata for a project scope.
quality_checks: Use project_id 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000 when the user did not name a different project.
Use only when host metadata is requested.
tools.use_when: Use when the user asks for host architecture, GPU, OS, or other host metadata aggregation.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid when the user names one specific dimension with a dedicated top endpoint.
tools.name: projects_events_aggregate_top_field_values
tools.purpose: Find common values across multiple common Zymtrace filter fields.
quality_checks: Use project_id 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000 when the user did not name a different project.
Prefer dedicated top endpoints for one exact dimension.
tools.use_when: Use when the user asks which filters or field values are available or common.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid for the normal default-project path. Do not search just because a project-scoped tool needs a project_id; use default project_id 00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000 unless the user explicitly asks otherwise.
tools.name: projects_search
tools.purpose: Find, list, or switch Zymtrace projects before calling tools for a non-default project.
quality_checks: Use only when the user asks to list/search projects or names a different project without a UUID.
Do not use for default project metrics, events, stats, or recommendations.
tools.use_when: Use when the user asks what projects are available, asks to find/search/list projects, or names a non-default project but does not provide its UUID.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid for runtime hot paths; use topfunctions, topentities, or hot_traces.
tools.name: projects_stats_aggregate_top_architectures
tools.purpose: Rank host CPU architectures for a known project.
tools.use_when: Use when the user asks which architectures are present or hottest.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid for pod, namespace, deployment, host, or container-specific ranking.
tools.name: projects_stats_aggregate_top_clusters
tools.purpose: Rank clusters for a known project.
tools.use_when: Use when the user asks which clusters are contributing most.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid for pod, namespace, deployment, host, or executable ranking.
tools.name: projects_stats_aggregate_top_containers
tools.purpose: Rank containers for a known project.
tools.use_when: Use when the user asks which containers are hottest or most active.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid for pod, namespace, container, host, or executable ranking.
tools.name: projects_stats_aggregate_top_deployments
tools.purpose: Rank deployments for a known project.
tools.use_when: Use when the user asks which deployments are hottest or most active.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid for host metadata details; use projects_events_aggregate_host_metadata then.
tools.name: projects_stats_aggregate_top_hosts
tools.purpose: Rank hosts for a known project.
tools.use_when: Use when the user asks which hosts are hottest or most active.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid for script, thread, container, pod, or function ranking.
tools.name: projects_stats_aggregate_top_main_exes
tools.purpose: Rank main executables for a known project.
tools.use_when: Use when the user asks which executables or processes are hottest.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid for pod, deployment, container, host, or executable ranking.
tools.name: projects_stats_aggregate_top_namespaces
tools.purpose: Rank Kubernetes namespaces for a known project.
tools.use_when: Use when the user asks which namespaces are hottest or most active.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid for namespace, deployment, container, host, or executable ranking.
tools.name: projects_stats_aggregate_top_pods
tools.purpose: Rank Kubernetes pods for a known project.
tools.use_when: Use when the user asks which pods are hottest or most active.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid for executable or function ranking.
tools.name: projects_stats_aggregate_top_script_names
tools.purpose: Rank script names for a known project.
tools.use_when: Use when the user asks which scripts are hottest or most active.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid for function ranking; use topfunctions for function names.
tools.name: projects_stats_aggregate_top_thread_names
tools.purpose: Rank thread names for a known project.
tools.use_when: Use when the user asks which threads are hottest or most active.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid for built-in dimensions such as host, pod, container, executable, or script.
tools.name: projects_stats_aggregate_top_user_tags
tools.purpose: Rank custom user tags for a known project.
tools.use_when: Use when the user asks which custom tags are hottest or most active.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid when the user asks for raw stack evidence first; use hot_traces or topfunctions to inspect evidence.
tools.name: recommendations
tools.purpose: Return already-generated actionable optimization recommendations for hot traces.
quality_checks: Use after or alongside hot-trace discovery when the user asks what to change.
Use a wider window such as 30 days when the user asks for existing recommendations without a time range.
Page only when more recommendations are needed.
tools.use_when: Use when the user asks for optimization recommendations, suggested fixes, or already-generated advice.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid when the user asks for function names, stack traces, call trees, or flamegraphs.

properties.entity: executable, script, host, thread, deployment, container, namespace, or pod

tools.name: topentities
tools.purpose: Rank top runtime entities such as executables, scripts, hosts, threads, deployments, containers, namespaces, pods, apps, services, or workloads.
quality_checks: Use the MCP topentities resource first when the host supports resources.
Set entity to the dimension the user named; for containerized apps choose entity=container.
Use CPU unless the user asks for GPU or allocation data.
Exclude zymtrace-profiler from optimization targets.
tools.use_when: Use for rank-first requests asking which host, container, pod, namespace, executable, script, deployment, app, service, workload, or thread is hottest or should be optimized first.

tools.avoid_when: Avoid when the user asks for hosts, containers, pods, scripts, raw traces, a call tree, or a flamegraph.
tools.name: topfunctions
tools.purpose: Rank hottest functions for CPU, GPU, or allocation profiles.
quality_checks: Use the MCP topfunctions resource first when the host supports resources.
Use CPU unless the user asks for GPU or allocation data.
Apply entity filters when the user names a host, container, pod, executable, or script.
tools.use_when: Use when the user asks which functions, methods, frames, or symbols are hottest, or for rank-first function summaries before a hot_traces drilldown.

result.description: Compare stock and tuned descriptions for the Zymtrace MCP profiling-analysis tools.

cases: default project metrics discovery skips search
full trace error recovers to discovery
gpu call tree uses hot traces
gpu inference workflow starts with metrics

results.case: default project metrics discovery skips search

choice.rationale: The task requests discovery of active metric names (GPU, CPU, model, service, token, latency) for a specific time range. According to the rules, GPU workflow should start with project_metrics_activity_aggr to discover matching GPU/framework/CPU metrics first. This tool is specifically designed to retrieve metrics activity aggregations and discover available metric names before querying their values.

results.case: cpu rank first containerized apps

choice.rationale: This is a rank-first CPU request asking which containerized app to optimize, which perfectly matches the topentities use case. According to the rules, 'Rank-first CPU requests such as what is eating CPU, which app/container/pod/process is hottest, or biggest ROI should start with topentities or topfunctions.' Since the user wants to know which container (not which function) is consuming the most CPU, topentities with entity=container is the correct choice.

results.case: gpu inference workflow starts with metrics

choice.rationale: This is a GPU/vLLM inference investigation request. According to the Zymtrace rules, GPU, CUDA, inference, vLLM, SGLang, Triton, training, or accelerator requests use the GPU workflow: discover matching GPU/framework/CPU metrics first with project_metrics_activity_aggr, then query metric values when names are known, then pull the GPU hot_traces view and the matching CPU hot_traces view with the same filters. Starting with project_metrics_activity_aggr will discover relevant GPU utilization, framework, and CPU metrics related to the vLLM workload during the specified time period.

results.case: gpu call tree uses hot traces

choice.rationale: This is a GPU workflow request for vLLM. According to the rules, GPU requests should use hot_traces for call-tree analysis. The task explicitly asks for 'first-pass discovery', which maps to the requirement that first hot_traces calls must use meta_only=true. The filter_text parameter helps narrow down to the vLLM workload. The limit parameter is not included but would default appropriately for discovery mode.

results.case: selected trace drilldown is bounded

choice.rationale: This is a full trace fetch request for a specific prefix_hash (981237). According to the rules, full trace fetches require a selected prefix_hash and should use limit=1. The user explicitly wants to drill into this specific trace, so meta_only should not be set (defaulting to false for full trace data).

results.case: full trace error recovers to discovery

choice.rationale: The task requires recovering from a failed hot_traces call by performing first-pass CPU hot trace discovery. According to the Zymtrace rules, first hot_traces calls must use meta_only=true and limit<=5. Since no limit is specified and the default should be safe, and no filter_text or prefix_hash is needed for discovery mode, the minimal required parameters are profile_type=CPU, the specified time range, and meta_only=true.

results.case: hot trace discovery is bounded

choice.rationale: The task requests hot trace candidates as metadata only for CPU profiling. According to the rules, first hot_traces calls must use meta_only=true and limit<=5. Since the user wants only 3 candidates, the meta_only flag will return lightweight metadata without full stack traces, allowing them to select a prefix_hash for subsequent detailed analysis.

results.case: resource fallback hot functions

choice.rationale: The task explicitly requests using the topfunctions resource to rank hottest CPU functions. According to the rules, rank-first CPU requests like 'what is eating CPU' or 'which function is hottest' should start with topfunctions. This is a direct function-ranking request for CPU profile data within the specified time range.

results.case: default project metrics discovery skips search

choice.rationale: The task asks to discover active metric names (GPU, CPU, model, service, token, latency) for the default project. According to the rules, GPU and inference investigations should start with project_metrics_activity_aggr to discover matching GPU/framework/CPU metrics first. This tool is specifically designed to discover active metric names and useful attributes including GPU, CPU, model, service, token, latency, and framework metrics. The user has provided absolute timestamps, so no time conversion is needed. Using default project_id since no other project was specified.

results.case: cpu rank first containerized apps

choice.rationale: The user is asking a rank-first CPU optimization question about containerized apps. According to the rules, 'CPU-only or entity-only investigations default to CPU' and 'Rank-first CPU requests such as what is eating CPU, which app/container/pod/process is hottest, or biggest ROI should start with topentities or topfunctions, then drill into the selected user-owned entry with hot_traces.' For containerized apps, the entity dimension should be 'container'. The user has provided explicit RFC3339 timestamps, so no time conversion is needed.

results.case: gpu inference workflow starts with metrics

choice.rationale: This is a GPU/inference investigation for vLLM workload. According to the rules, GPU, inference, vLLM requests use the GPU workflow which starts with project_metrics_activity_aggr to discover matching GPU/framework/CPU metrics first. This will identify all relevant GPU utilization, framework, model, service, token, and latency metrics before querying their values or pulling hot traces. The user has provided absolute RFC3339 timestamps which convert to Unix milliseconds: 2026-06-25T04:00:00+00:00 = 1750647600000 and 2026-06-25T05:00:00+00:00 = 1750651200000.

results.case: gpu call tree uses hot traces

choice.rationale: The task asks for a GPU call tree for the vLLM workload with first-pass discovery only. According to the rules, GPU requests should use hot_traces for call-tree analysis, and the first hot_traces call must use meta_only=true with limit<=5. The filter_text parameter is used to target the vLLM workload specifically. This will return trace metadata without full stack details, allowing the user to select a prefix_hash for deeper drilldown in a subsequent call.

results.case: selected trace drilldown is bounded

choice.rationale: The user is requesting a full drilldown into a specific trace by prefix_hash. According to the rules, when drilling into a selected prefix_hash, hot_traces should be called with meta_only=false and limit=1. The task explicitly provides the prefix_hash (981237), profile type (CPU), and time window, making this a full trace fetch operation rather than an initial discovery.

results.case: full trace error recovers to discovery

choice.rationale: The task requires recovery from a failed hot_traces call that attempted to fetch full stacks without a selected prefix_hash. Following Zymtrace rules, the first hot_traces call must use meta_only=true with a small limit (≤5) for discovery. This will return trace metadata including prefix_hash values that can be used in subsequent drilldown requests. Using CPU profile_type as the default, the specified time window, and limit=5 for initial discovery.

results.case: hot trace discovery is bounded

choice.rationale: The user explicitly requests hot trace candidates as metadata only (not full stacks) for CPU profiling. According to the rules, the first hot_traces call must use meta_only=true and limit<=5. The user wants top 3 candidates, so limit=3 is appropriate. This is a discovery phase before drilling into a selected prefix_hash.

The time window is already provided in RFC3339 format, no additional filters are specified, and no limit is mentioned so the default will apply.
